Plaintiffs stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for plaintiffs (both appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the stipulation form on their behalf.

The Court notes that counsel for defendant Lyft did not properly contest the Court's tentative ruling as is required under Local Rule 8.3(D).

Matter discussed and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with the sole modification of changing the deadline by which the deposition is to take place, as follows:

Motion is granted in part. Lyft shall designate and produce for deposition the person most qualified to testify on its behalf regarding topics 1-3. The deposition shall take place on a mutually convenient date before September 9, 2022. Lyft also shall produce any documents responsive to document request nos. 1, 2 and 3, to the extent those documents have not already been produced after a reasonable inquiry of everyone who might be holding responsive documents or who knows where such documents might be held. Maldonado v. Superior Court (ICG Telecom Group, Inc.) (2002) 94 Cal.App.4th 1390, 1396. Before commencement of the deposition Lyft shall provide a sufficiently detailed privilege log for any information withheld on attorney-client and/or work product privilege grounds. If necessary, Ms. Shapirshteyn and Mr. Casey shall meet and confer in good faith, either in person or via Zoom, regarding the scope of any topics or requests. No sanctions as to this motion but sanctions may be awarded against either party if the motion is renewed. In the future counsel are reminded to provide courtesy copies pursuant to Local Rule 2.7(B).

Counsel for plaintiffs is to prepare and submit a proposed order to the pro tem judge at aaron@minnisandsmallets.com.
